headline: Zazzle Names Brian Yick Chief Strategy Officer
summary: MENLO PARK, Calif., June 23, 2026--Zazzle, the platform where ideas become real things that create lasting moments, today announced that Brian Yick has joined the company as Chief Strategy Officer. In the role, Yick will lead business and corporate development and help drive the company's growth as a technology platform.

content.title: Zazzle Names Brian Yick Chief Strategy Officer
content.summary: MENLO PARK, Calif., June 23, 2026--Zazzle, the platform where ideas become real things that create lasting moments, today announced that Brian Yick has joined the company as Chief Strategy Officer. headline: GameStop drops Cohen’s $35B pay plan to focus on eBay acquisition
summary: Investing.com -- GameStop Corp. said Tuesday its board has approved Chairman and CEO Ryan Cohen's request to withdraw a proposed CEO Performance Award from the company's proxy statement as the retailer shifts focus to its planned acquisition of eBay Inc.

content.title: GameStop drops Cohen’s $35B pay plan to focus on eBay acquisition
content.summary: Investing.com -- GameStop Corp. said Tuesday its board has approved Chairman and CEO Ryan Cohen's request to withdraw a proposed CEO Performance Award from the company's proxy statement as the retailer shifts focus to its planned acquisition of eBay Inc.

headline: GameStop CEO Cohen spurns $35 billion pay plan to focus on plan to buy eBay
summary: Ryan Cohen, the billionaire CEO of GameStop, will not receive a potential performance award from the ‌videogame retailer and plans to unveil more details about his bid to ‌take over eBay soon, the company said on Tuesday.  GameStop unveiled a compensation package worth roughly $35 billion for Cohen ​in January, hinging on a turnaround that requires him to lift the struggling company's market value more than tenfold and sharply boost its profit.  In May, Cohen surprised Wall Street with an unsolicited offer to buy eBay for roughly $56 billion in cash and stock to ‌turn the e-commerce company into ⁠a bigger competitor to Amazon.

content.title: GameStop CEO Cohen spurns $35 billion pay plan to focus on plan to buy eBay
content.summary: Ryan Cohen, the billionaire CEO of GameStop, will not receive a potential performance award from the ‌videogame retailer and plans to unveil more details about his bid to ‌take over eBay soon, the company said on Tuesday.  GameStop unveiled a compensation package worth roughly $35 billion for Cohen ​in January, hinging on a turnaround that requires him to lift the struggling company's market value more than tenfold and sharply boost its profit.  In May, Cohen surprised Wall Street with an unsolicited offer to buy eBay for roughly $56 billion in cash and stock to ‌turn the e-commerce company into ⁠a bigger competitor to Amazon.

headline: Ryan Cohen Withdraws CEO Performance Award; GameStop Focuses on eBay
summary: GRAPEVINE, Texas, June 23, 2026--GameStop Corp. (NYSE: GME) ("GameStop" or the "Company") today announced that its Board of Directors (the "Board") has granted the request of Ryan Cohen, Chairman and CEO, to amend the Company's proxy statement to remove the proposed CEO Performance Award. When the Board approved the CEO Performance Award in January 2026, the Company had not yet decided to pursue the acquisition of eBay, Inc. ("eBay").

content.title: Ryan Cohen Withdraws CEO Performance Award; GameStop Focuses on eBay
content.summary: GRAPEVINE, Texas, June 23, 2026--GameStop Corp. (NYSE: GME) ("GameStop" or the "Company") today announced that its Board of Directors (the "Board") has granted the request of Ryan Cohen, Chairman and CEO, to amend the Company's proxy statement to remove the proposed CEO Performance Award. When the Board approved the CEO Performance Award in January 2026, the Company had not yet decided to pursue the acquisition of eBay, Inc. ("eBay").
